title: rosa sepium f. arvatica
intro: Rosa sepium f. arvatica, also known as Rosa rugosa, is a species of rose native to East Asia, characterized by its fragrant flowers and edible hips. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, to treat digestive issues, fever, and inflammation due to its high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. The hips of Rosa sepium f. arvatica are rich in vitamin C, making them a valuable source of nutrition, and they have been used to promote cardiovascular health, boost the immune system, and reduce the risk of certain cancers. Rosa sepium f. arvatica can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed fresh or dried, and the plant's oil can also be extracted for topical use.
intro_study: According to "Voprosy pitaniia", rosa sepium f. arvatica benefits include a high antioxidant effect, specifically, the Desertniy variety shows the highest indicator of antiradical activity and restoring force, which can help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage.
